_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Acute onset of fever, confusion, disorientation

preauth.label: Clinical notes including history, evaluation findings, vital monitoring, and planned line of management
Optional (Based on suspected etiology): Viral serology markers, Liver function tests, Metabolic panel, Chest X-ray, USG abdomen

claim.label: Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) (including any cross-specialty referral that has been done)

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes detail history, signs & symptoms, vital monitoring (HR, RR, BP), planned treatment line, and advice for admission?
Imaging and/or investigations confirm the etiology of encephalopathy diagnosis?
Patient complained of nausea, lethargy, seizures or focal neurological dysfunction?
Imaging and/or investigations confirm the diagnosis of encephalopathy?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs (including any cross-specialty referral) with daily vitals and treatment details submitted?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advice at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Patient did not complain of nausea, lethargy, seizures or focal neurological dysfunction.
Imaging and/or investigations do not confirm the diagnosis of encephalopathy.
Clinical notes are incomplete or missing vital monitoring, planned treatment, or admission advice.
Required Lumbar Puncture report is missing for pre-authorization.
Required Blood culture report is missing for pre-authorization.
Required Complete blood count report is missing for pre-authorization.
Required CT/MRI scan report is missing for pre-authorization.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) are missing or lack daily vitals and treatment details.
Discharge summary with follow-up advice is missing.

additional_information.min_doctor_qualification_desirable: MD/DNB/DCH/Equivalent (Pediatrics), MD/DNB/Equivalent (General Medicine), DM/DNB/Equivalent (Neurology), Neurosurgical referral if required
clinical_key_pointers: Meningitis is an inflammation of thin membrane of brain and spinal cord. Most common in children caused by viral or bacterial infection and moves into cerebral spinal fluid. Also caused by parasite or fungus usually from respiratory tract.
Causes: Viral, Bacterial, Parasitic, Non-Infectious, Aseptic
